title: error locating item named "manifest" for image with ID .... when parallel pulling images and listing them
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I pull multiple images in parallel and at the same time invoke `podman image ls` the later sometimes fails with an error like
```
Error: error locating item named "manifest" for image with ID "73cdb17d7c55ea37c6d9b24adb900bc0fbff828b179e1ff04bb0fe735743ba13": file does not exist
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman image prune --all -f`

2. start `while podman image ls; do echo ""; done` in one terminal window

3. pull multiple images in parallel in another window. in my test I pulled 4 images with around 5 layers each from a docker registry running on the same host.

**Describe the results you received:**
all pull requests to work and no `podman image ls` call to fail.

**Describe the results you expected:**
sometimes the image listing fails with the above error. I was so far only able to reproduce this once, so it does not fail all the time.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I'm using the vfs storage driver.
As stated the issue only happens occasionally.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.14
Built:        Tue Oct 19 13:00:58 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body: @vrothberg This is a corrupted c/storage situation, right?

If the storage was really corrupted I would expect that it stays that way. To me it looks like the image listing code is reading data from the container storage that is not correctly locked by some lock file and thus sees some image data that it should not see (yet).

> @vrothberg This is a corrupted c/storage situation, right?

Probably, yes. What I "suspect" is a race between pruning (removing image data) and looking up image data.
```
process 1      lookup image                            inspect manifest
process 2                          remove image
time           ---------------------------------------------------------------->
```

Looking up an image and inspecting it is not an atomic operation, so I think we have to optimize the image-listing code path to make these errors non-fatal as we've done for other code paths already.
I'm actually doing the pruning before the parallel pulls. But the problem should be still the same following your description.

> I'm actually doing the pruning before the parallel pulls. But the problem should be still the same following your description.

If both commands run in parallel, the upper race would still apply.  I need to check further though since we have optimized many code paths for such errors already.

Found the location: https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/libimage/filters.go#L34-L36

We need to add some further error checks here when filtering images.  There is a number of PR in flight extending the filters.  Once merged, I'll prepare a PR to fix this issue.
